authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 111, Customs Brokers
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary customs-broker regulation covering broker duties, records, responsible supervision and control, and required client notification when the broker knows of client noncompliance or document error.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Broker-client request handling and corrective-action boundary

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 163, Recordkeeping
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary customs recordkeeping rule that defines when customs records must exist, be retained, and be produced, which directly constrains what client service can confirm as authoritative.

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 141, Entry of Merchandise
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative entry workflow rule set for importer liability, right to make entry, and power-of-attorney context that establishes the account and transaction truth set behind client requests.

authoritative_sources.title: Customs Brokers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CBP program page for broker licensing and administration, used to anchor service ownership, broker program terminology, and permit-related escalation paths.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Client request ownership and broker-program routing

authoritative_sources.title: Tips for New Importers and Exporters
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CBP importer guidance that clarifies the broker-versus-importer responsibility line and supports safe customer communication about documentation and duties.

authoritative_sources.title: Reconciliation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CBP reconciliation workflow reference for post-entry adjustments and data finalization, used to determine when service work must stop and post-entry handling must begin.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Post-entry discrepancy handling and owner transfer
used_for: client expectation setting for unresolved adjustments

authoritative_sources.title: FinCEN Advisory FIN-2010-A001 on Trade-Based Money Laundering
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. financial-crime guidance for trade-based money laundering red flags relevant to unusual invoice changes, third-party payments, and suspicious document patterns raised in broker-client service channels.

authoritative_sources.title: A Framework for OFAC Compliance Commitments
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of Foreign Assets Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative sanctions-compliance framework used to define escalation and control expectations when client requests intersect with sanctions screening or denied-party concerns.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 20022 Financial Services Messaging Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized standard for payment and reconciliation vocabulary used to normalize duty, broker-fee, remittance, and settlement artifacts across CRM, payment, and GL systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Payment artifact normalization and reconciliation review

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and identity or case grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the client, importer, entry, shipment, and case identifiers needed to safely act and establish whether the request is informational, complaint-driven, exception-driven, or a handoff.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Record-of-truth and evidence retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Verify broker notes, customs entry records, payment or duty records, notices, and communication history before stating status or next steps.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Supported request resolution and safe communication
workflow_stages.description: Respond using only documented workflow state, document requirements, and ownership boundaries without promising approvals, legal outcomes, or exceptions.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Exception, complaint, and repeat-failure escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package complaints, unsupported asks, unresolved document gaps, and repeat service failures for the correct owner with an auditable communication trail.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, sanctions, and reconciliation conflict handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when third-party payment patterns, invoice inconsistencies, sanctions concerns, or unreconciled broker-fee or duty records materially change the answer.

workflow_stages.name: Policy conflict and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: When tenant policy imposes stricter complaint, refund, or communication controls than the public baseline, record the adaptation and stop short of unsupported synthesis.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

artifacts: Client request, callback, and communication logs
Importer of record, power of attorney, and broker account records
Entry numbers, entry summary extracts, and CBP notices
Invoices, packing lists, bills of lading, and deficiency notices
Broker fee schedules, remittance records, duty statements, and GL or payment extracts
KYC, AML, sanctions, fraud, dispute, and approval-history artifacts
systems_of_record: ACE or tenant customs filing platform
Document management repository for customs and client records
Fraud monitoring, KYC/AML, and sanctions screening systems
Tenant approval-routing and complaint tracking systems

allowed: Resolve informational broker-client requests when the request can be answered from authoritative records and documented workflow rules.
Request minimum missing identifiers and documents needed to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed communication summaries, complaint notes, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
Route the case to the correct adjacent owner when the workflow becomes post-entry, customs legal, fraud/AML, sanctions, or discretionary approval work.
escalate: Required tenant facts, identity context, or source records are missing or contradictory.
The request seeks a policy exception, refund, fee waiver, or release promise beyond documented service authority.
Fraud, AML, sanctions, consumer-impact, or reconciliation conflict signals appear.
Public guidance and tenant policy materially disagree on what can be communicated or promised.
refuse: Requests for customs legal interpretation, sanctions interpretation, or final regulatory advice.
Requests to fabricate approvals, confirm unresolved payment settlement, or assert unsupported entry status.
Requests to bypass documented complaint, approval, or fraud-escalation controls.

domain_failure_modes: Telling the client a shipment is cleared or a correction is accepted when ACE and internal records are stale or contradictory.
Treating a broker-service request as authority to approve refunds, fee credits, or post-entry changes.
Missing TBML or sanctions red flags because a service request is framed as simple payment or document help.
Using generic customer-service language that hides importer-of-record responsibility or evidence dependencies.
Closing repeat complaints without an owner-routed escalation package and communication log.
Resolving a billing or duty dispute without reconciling CRM, payment, and GL evidence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific threshold for when broker-fee credits may be discussed with the client
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Treat missing tenant thresholds as escalation-only and do not imply approval authority.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether a tenant routes post-entry discrepancy questions first to customs operations or a dedicated drawback/post-entry team
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require tenant owner map at runtime and return orchestrator-safe routing when the owner is unspecified.
